rulings.Case Title: MAJUA & NOYON LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MG2 LLC, A WYOMING LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment On It
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER MAJUA & NOYON LLC's Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment On It. Hearing required to coordinate hearings on petitions to confirm and vacate arbitration award. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CLEAR HOMES LLC, A NEW MEXICO LIMITED LIABILITY VS. BRENDAN MICHAEL WEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 2. DEFENDANTS ALLYSON GROSS, LYNDEN KUWADA, RILEY PARSONS, AND STEVEN MYERS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the unverified first amended complaint (FAC) is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff acknowledges the FAC makes unclear and/or equivocal allegations, but such statements do "not create a binding judicial admission." (Stroud v. Tunsi (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 377, 385.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. CHRISTIAN LARSEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission As Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANT RIPPLE LABS INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission As Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion is denied as moot. Based on the parties' conduct, the stay only applies to prosecution of the complaint. The Weberman declaration shows that plaintiff did not receive the request for admissions. The motion is moot since she subsequently received the discovery request and responded. The parties are to meet and confer regarding whether the response is code compliant or plaintiff should provide an amended response. No sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE, N.A. VS. KIANA HARROD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 7. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE, N.A.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Off calendar. The Carr declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred in compliance with CCP 439. In addition, the basis for the motion is that defendant filed an answer admitting plaintiff's allegations. There is no answer on file with the court based on a review of the court's register of actions. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FABIOLA SALMERON REYES ET AL VS. INTEGRATED RESOURCES GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, December 11, 2024, Line 8. DEFENDANT CAESARSTONE USA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The motion fails to show a meet and confer in compliance with CCP 435.5. The motion refers to the Brice declaration but no such declaration is filed with the court. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
